mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 200 index points, monetary policy is restrictive. This suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% and CPIAUCSL at 201.7 both exceed thresholds
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This depresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.5% is at the threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, indicating weakening consumer demand. This signals reduced corporate revenue growth and earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS growth is below 0.5%, or Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of periods when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3% month-over-month, or Rank IC falls below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1400 thousand units while simultaneously the unemployment rate rises above 4.3%, indicating a joint demand shock in both residential investment and labor markets. This dual weakness compresses aggregate demand and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1486.0 and UNRATE at 4.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are triggered, or the joint effect is not significantly more predictive than either signal alone (interaction F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand persons per month, signaling labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. This allows the Federal Reserve to pause or cut rates, compressing discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 100 thousand persons
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k, or the interaction term between PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling a shift toward monetary easing. This reduces the cost of capital for corporations and increases present value of future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.5pp over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances following a 0.5pp decline in FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously contract month-over-month, indicating a synchronized demand shock across both consumption and overall economic activity. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11450.5 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 368861.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint predictive power is not significantly greater than either signal alone (interaction F-test p > 0.15).
